results.possibleIdentity: Marcial Rodriguez (often referred to as Marcial Rodriguez-Brossoise) was a technical professional and horticulture expert active in New York City during the late 1960s and 1970s. He was associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization that facilitated collaborations between artists and engineers. Rodriguez was registered in the E.A.T. technical services registry, where he listed expertise in a diverse range of fields including biology, botany, horticulture, stage lighting, light gardening, and light equipment/projection. Professionally, he was highly regarded in the bonsai community; he was a member of the New York Bonsai Society and an instructor at national bonsai conventions, such as the 1973 joint BCI/ABS convention in Atlanta. He authored the "Bonsai" chapter in the multi-volume craft and hobby series 'The Family Creative Workshop' (published by Plenary Publications International in New York, 1974–1976). His unique combination of technical skills in lighting and projection with biological and horticultural knowledge made him a specialized resource for artists interested in environmental and light-based plant works.
